creator: tatsuki_makino@hotmail.com
text:
To begin with, version 2.3.0 seems to have not worked even with polkit enabled.
The part where the code changes when polkit is enabled is not used because of the following conditions.

#if defined(HAVE_POLKIT) && defined(SO_PEERCRED)

Since 2.3.1, this problem has been caused by the fact that it works properly using LOCAL_PEERCRED code.
So a workaround using --disable-polkit to get the same behavior as 2.3.0 would be a good one.

It may be better to do some preliminary work in order to use 2.3.1 with polkit enabled.
It would be nice to have a more generic version of what I have already written in comment #13.
The following functions can be used to restrict the users who can use pcsc-lite actions to only those who belong to the pcsc-lite group.

polkit.addRule(
    function (action, subject) {
        if ((action.id == "org.debian.pcsc-lite.access_card" ||
            action.id == "org.debian.pcsc-lite.access_pcsc") &&
            subject.isInGroup("pcsc-lite")) {
            return polkit.Result.YES;
        }
    });

All that's left to do is create a pcsc-lite group and add all the users who will use pcsc reader to it.

I have already written that it's better to terminate polkitd with sigint after rewriting the rules file, but it seems that a new process can be started with -r option to take over the role.

/usr/local/lib/polkit-1/polkitd -r -l debug


polkitd started by dbus with the contents of /usr/local/share/dbus-1/system-services/org.freedesktop.PolicyKit1.service have a poor amount of logging, so changing the log-level will output more logs.

The arguments passed to the rule function are as follows, and it seems that complicated things cannot be used.

[Action id='org.debian.pcsc-lite.access_pcsc']
[Subject pid=18212 user='tatsuki' groups=tatsuki,video,webcamd seat=null session=null system_unit=null local=false active=false]

[Action id='org.debian.pcsc-lite.access_card' reader='SmartCard Reader [Vendor Interface] 00 00']
[Subject pid=18212 user='tatsuki' groups=tatsuki,video,webcamd seat=null session=null system_unit=null local=false active=false]

Today, I looked into something like this :)
